name: Puente Pozo de Almoguera y el Monumento a los Ojos - by Mondéjar — circular from Hontoba
name_original: Puente Pozo de Almoguera y el Monumento a los Ojos - por Mondéjar — circular desde Hontoba
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hontoba traverse a landscape characterized by the deep valley of the San Sebastián stream and the Tajuña River, within the broader La Alcarria region. This area features open landscapes, tranquil roads, and gentle hills, offering varied terrain for road cyclists. The Tajuña River valley provides scenic routes through limestone canyons, orchards, and meadows, while the La Alcarria region presents a mix of ascents and more relaxed stretches. Best road cycling routes around Hontoba The most popular road cycling route is Puente Pozo de Almoguera y el Monumento a los Ojos - by Mondéjar — circular from Hontoba, a 68.2 miles (109.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores diverse landscapes, including river valleys and open countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pastrana and Zorita de los Canes Castle Loop, a moderate 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain and includes historical points of interest such as castles. Local road cyclists also love the Pioz Castle – Church of San Pedro (Hontoba) loop from Hontoba, a 42.3 miles (68.0 km) trail leading through the La Alcarria region and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Hontoba is defined by the Tajuña River valley, the open landscapes of La Alcarria, and varied elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Hontoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 5 reviews. More than 280 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hontoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Hontoba?
A: Road cycling around Hontoba offers a diverse landscape. You'll find routes traversing the deep valley of the San Sebastián stream and the Tajuña River, characterized by limestone canyons, orchards, and meadows. The broader La Alcarria region provides open landscapes, tranquil roads, and gentle hills, with varied elevations that include both challenging ascents and more relaxed stretches.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Hontoba?
A: The routes in Hontoba cater to various abilities. Out of 24 available routes, 15 are classified as moderate, offering a good balance of challenge and enjoyment. For those seeking more demanding rides, there are 9 difficult routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can encounter several points of interest, such as the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which showcases historical architectural design. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3501590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, an Asset of Heritage Interest, is also linked to traditional livestock farming routes. Additionally, the ruins of the ancient medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4017473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de los Llanos&lt;/a&gt; offer splendid views.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Hontoba?
A: The road cycling routes in Hontoba are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from 5 reviews. Over 280 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty of the Tajuña River valley and the tranquil roads of La Alcarria.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Hontoba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31739396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle – Church of San Pedro (Hontoba) loop from Hontoba&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 68 km and leads through the La Alcarria region past historical landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Hontoba?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and often clear skies. During late spring and early summer, you might even catch the beautiful lavender fields in bloom around the broader Guadalajara province, adding a visually stunning element to your ride.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Tajuña River?
A: Absolutely. The Tajuña River Valley is a significant feature of the region, and several routes allow you to immerse yourself in its natural beauty. The "Road Along the Tajuña River" is a scenic route that offers picturesque landscapes and opportunities to discover historical elements along its banks, flowing through gorges, limestone canyons, orchards, and poplar groves.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists in Hontoba?
A: While specific designated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, Hontoba is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village. It's advisable to look for public parking spaces or consult local information upon arrival.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: While many routes in Hontoba are moderate to difficult, the tranquil roads and gentle hills of the La Alcarria region can offer more relaxed stretches suitable for families with some cycling experience. It's recommended to check the specific route profiles for distance and elevation to choose one that matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink in Hontoba after a ride?
A: Hontoba, being a smaller village, will have local establishments where you can find refreshments. For a wider selection of cafes and restaurants, you might consider nearby towns along the Tajuña River, such as Cifuentes or Brihuega, which are often incorporated into longer cycling routes.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes with significant elevation gain for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Hontoba offers several challenging routes for experienced riders. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1015143481/puente-pozo-de-almoguera-y-el-monumento-a-los-ojos-by-mondejar-circular-from-hontoba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Pozo de Almoguera y el Monumento a los Ojos - by Mondéjar — circular from Hontoba&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult and features over 1,100 meters of elevation gain over its 109.8 km distance, exploring diverse landscapes including river valleys and open countryside.

Q: What is the overall number of road cycling routes available around Hontoba?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Hontoba, offering a wide variety of options for exploring the region's natural beauty and historical sites.
tips: It is important to know that the section just before the Greenway is likely to get muddy during the rainy season. there is an alternative by another path that I explored afterwards in case we used it on the way back and that is safer in that sense

Mondéjar has the guarantee of the Designation of Origin of its wines (in 2016, the harvest was three million kilos of grapes). Cultural things that may interest you: The parish church of Santa María Magdalena, the hermitage of San Sebastián and the convent of San Antonio (although in ruins).
Very interesting, I mark it for my planning, thank you very much.

Villar del Olmo Town Hall has a QR code system, and you can discover the 21 points of interest, both scenic and historical, through your mobile or tablet, with information and videos from a drone view. There are many routes and many places of interest.
